summary: local-mcp performs install-time download-and-execute from non-publisher endpoint with system info beacon
details: The package's setup.js, index.js, and download.js together form an install-time dropper and beacon. download.js fetches binaries via https.get from hosts including https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app and https://download.local-mcp.com and writes them to disk via fs (download.js around lines 8-13, 92, 171, 204). index.js reads system identity from os.homedir() and process.env/process.platform (lines 16, 71, 189, 286, 23) and issues outbound HTTP/HTTPS calls (http.get line 109, https.get line 203) to https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app, while invoking child_process (line 14) to execute the fetched content. setup.js issues multiple POST requests (lines 61, 343, 800, 878, 904) and likewise loads child_process, fs, os, and https. The endpoint office-mcp-production.up.railway.app is an anonymous Railway-hosted host that does not match any documented publisher domain, the fetched payload is executed via child_process at install time, and host metadata (homedir, env, platform) is collected and beaconed out — matching the install-time-RCE + sysinfo-exfiltration fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
